name: Glycolic acid
lead: The most-studied alpha-hydroxy acid in dermatology. Smallest molecule, deepest penetration, strongest evidence on tone and texture — and the steepest irritation curve to match. The default home AHA when used at a sensible dose, the workhorse of in-clinic peels at higher ones.

whatItIs.body: lycolic acid is the smallest alpha-hydroxy acid by molecular weight, derived originally from sugarcane. Its size lets it move past the corneocyte surface and reach the lower stratum corneum and viable epidermis — which is why it produces the most visible AHA result and the most predictable AHA irritation. There is no separating those two facts.

mechanism.b: Cleaves corneodesmosomes between stratum-corneum cells. Visible smoother texture within 2 weeks.
Stimulates fibroblast proliferation and dermal glycosaminoglycan synthesis at sustained use.
Accelerates turnover of pigmented keratinocytes; useful adjunct for PIH but not first-line for melasma.

evidence.note: 5–10% leave-on improves roughness and crepeyness reproducibly over 12 weeks.
Slower than retinoids on collagen endpoints; cleaner side-effect profile.
Useful adjunct. Stack with niacinamide or tranexamic for results that hold.
20–70% peels reproducibly improve photoaging endpoints. Operator-dependent.

concentration.b: Twice weekly to start. Most home users live here.
Nightly or alternate nights. Plateau dose for home use.
For experienced skin only. Rising irritation, marginal benefit.

pairings.note: Wait 20–30 min after AHA for pH to normalise, then layer.
Both pH-dependent and acidic. Stagger AM C, PM AHA.
Compounded irritation. Alternate nights or pick a single tool.
AHAs increase UV sensitivity for up to a week post-use.

products.name: Glow Tonic 5%
products.note: The reference beginner AHA. Honest pH, gentle vehicle.

products.name: Glycolic Acid 7% Toning Solution
products.note: Cheap, effective, drying for some. Skip the package warning at your peril.

products.name: T.L.C. Framboos 12%
products.note: Mixed-acid premium product. Good vehicle, premium markup.

products.name: Liquid Gold 5%
products.note: The cult classic. 12 weeks in, the texture difference is undeniable.

faq.a: Glycolic for established users wanting maximum exfoliation. Lactic for sensitive skin or anyone who also wants hydration. Different tools for adjacent jobs.

faq.q: How often can I use it?
faq.a: Twice weekly to start; build to nightly only if your barrier tolerates it. The 'every night' pitch on the bottle is aspirational, not prescriptive.

faq.q: Do I need sunscreen the next day?
faq.a: Yes, more than usual. AHAs measurably increase UV-induced damage for up to a week. Skip sunscreen and you have actively made things worse.
Adjunct only. Tranexamic acid, hydroquinone, and azelaic are first-line. Use glycolic to support turnover, not to drive the result.
